note: Computed Field UI is the admin-UI submodule of computed_field. It adds Field-UI integration (an 'Add computed field' action, edit/delete forms, and a report) but no config of its own, so it gets recipe-only cases graded on response text.

evals.prompt: What does the `computed_field_ui` submodule add to a Drupal site, and which two modules must it depend on? Does it store any configuration of its own?
evals.expected_output: It adds a UI over the parent computed_field module — an 'Add computed field' local action on each bundle's Manage fields page, plus edit/delete forms and a computed-fields report. It depends on computed_field and core field_ui, and provides no config of its own (saving the form creates a computed_field config entity from the parent module).

evals.prompt: With `computed_field_ui` enabled, how does a site builder add a computed field to the Article content type through the admin UI? What is the URL path of the add form?
evals.expected_output: Go to the Article Manage fields page and click the 'Add computed field' local action; the form is at {field_ui_base}/fields/add-computed-field, e.g. /admin/structure/types/manage/article/fields/add-computed-field. Pick a computed field plugin, set a label, and save.

evals.prompt: Where can I see a list of every computed field on a site running `computed_field_ui`, and what permission and machine-name prefix govern fields created through the UI?
evals.expected_output: The 'Computed fields' report at /admin/reports/fields/computed lists them all. Managing them requires the 'administer computed_field entities' permission, and new field machine names get the computed_field.settings:field_prefix prefix (default computed_).
